page: Amazon Web Services r5d.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 16 virtual CPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU.

This instance includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-speed local instance store volumes directly attached to the hardware. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, consistently rank in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also demonstrate average performance.

Given the high memory density and local NVMe SSD storage, the r5d.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-bound data processing tasks. However, it is less optimal for workloads heavily reliant on single-threaded CPU performance or high-speed network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 8 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 platform includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the bottom 25%, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations perform in the average middle 50% tier. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications requiring high-speed local storage, such as database servers and caching layers, though it is less suited for single-threaded compute-bound workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory
600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads
